title: Why is alcohol not under GST in India?
question: Why is alcohol not under GST in India?
angle: Alcohol for human consumption is constitutionally left to states as a revenue source, so it sits outside GST and is taxed via state excise — the single biggest reason prices differ across states.

title: Why do liquor prices vary so much across Indian states?
question: Why is the same bottle cheaper in one state than another?
angle: Because each state sets its own excise duty, retail model (private vs. state monopoly) and licence fees, the same bottle can cost very differently across a state line.

title: What is a standard drink, a peg, a large and a patiala peg?
question: How much alcohol is in a peg, large or patiala peg?
angle: Plain-English guide to Indian serving measures and the concept of a standard drink, so people actually know how much they're drinking.

title: How do bars price cocktails? Pour cost explained
question: Why are cocktails so expensive at bars?
angle: Bars price on pour cost, rent, staff and experience — the liquid is often the smallest part of what you pay for.

title: Why can't alcohol be advertised in India? Surrogate advertising explained
question: Why do liquor brands advertise water, soda and music?
angle: Direct alcohol advertising is banned, so brands advertise 'surrogate' products (water, soda, music, events) to keep the brand visible legally.

title: What is premiumization and why is it reshaping India's drinks market?
question: Why are premium Indian spirits and single malts booming?
angle: Rising incomes, aspiration and 'made in India' pride are pushing consumers toward higher-priced, higher-margin premium and craft spirits.

title: Which states in India are dry? Prohibition explained
question: Where is alcohol banned in India?
angle: A clear rundown of prohibition (dry) states and the economics and enforcement realities behind alcohol bans.

title: Does branding actually change how alcohol tastes?
question: Can you really taste an expensive whisky vs a cheap one?
angle: Expectation and priming shape perceived taste — branding does real perceptual work, which is exactly why it commands a price premium.

title: Traditional and tribal alcoholic drinks of India
question: What are the traditional and tribal alcoholic drinks of India?
angle: From mahua and handia to feni and toddy, India has a deep heritage of community-brewed drinks — culturally significant, often outside the formal excise economy, and increasingly being formalised and premiumised.

title: Apong, Zutho & Tongba: the traditional drinks of Northeast India
question: What alcohol do the tribes of Northeast India drink?
angle: The Northeast has a rich culture of rice beers and millet brews — apong, zutho, kyat, tongba and more — each tied to specific communities, festivals and rituals.
